An update from Eastnine AB ( (SE:EAST) ) is now available.

Bertrandt ( (DE:BDT) ) just unveiled an update.

Bertrandt AG has announced the planned publication dates for its upcoming group financial reports for the 2025/2026 fiscal year. The company will release its half-year figures on 13 May 2026 and its full-year results on 17 December 2026 via its investor relations website, providing investors and other stakeholders with a clear timetable for key financial disclosures.

By setting out its reporting schedule well in advance, Bertrandt offers greater visibility and planning certainty for analysts and shareholders. The transparent timing of the half-year and annual reports helps the capital market prepare for potential updates on the company’s business performance and strategic development in the coming fiscal periods.

Bertrandt AG is a Germany-based engineering services provider focused on the automotive and high-tech industries. AG Barr ( (GB:BAG) ) just unveiled an update.

A.G. Barr p.l.c. has published its annual report and accounts for the year ended 31 January 2026, following the release of its full-year financial results on 31 March 2026. The documentation includes details for the company’s one hundred and twenty-second annual general meeting, scheduled to take place in Glasgow on 22 May 2026.

The annual report, AGM notice and proxy form are now accessible via the company’s website, and the report will also be available on the UK National Storage Mechanism in line with listing and disclosure requirements. This step formalises the company’s annual governance cycle, giving shareholders and other stakeholders full access to regulated information ahead of the AGM.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:BAG) stock is a Hold with a £6.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on AG Barr stock, see the GB:BAG Stock Forecast page.

The main constraint is mixed-to-weak technical momentum (negative MACD and trading below longer moving averages) alongside recent free-cash-flow softness and a 2026 step-up in debt.

data.title: Shinelong Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Dividend, Board Changes and Capital Mandates
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Shinelong Automotive Lightweight Application Limited ( (HK:1930) ).

Shinelong Automotive Lightweight Application Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 11 June 2026 in Kunshan, Jiangsu Province, to present its 2025 audited results and seek shareholder approval for a final dividend of HK0.688 cents per share. Shareholders will vote on the re-election of two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, confirm directors’ remuneration, and consider the re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor.

The meeting will also ask investors to approve standard general mandates authorizing the board to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares and to issue up to 20% new shares, subject to Hong Kong listing rules and other conditions. These mandates are designed to give the board flexibility in managing capital structure and funding options, potentially influencing future share buy-backs, equity issuance, and overall capital management strategy for stakeholders.

More about Shinelong Automotive Lightweight Application Limited

Shinelong Automotive Lightweight Application Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the automotive sector with a focus on lightweight applications. Its business centers on supplying components and solutions that reduce vehicle weight, targeting automakers seeking improved efficiency and performance in mainland China and broader regional markets.

Average Trading Volume: 256,320

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$260.7M

data.description:

An announcement from Huayi Tencent Entertainment Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0419) ) is now available.

Hony Media Group has called its annual general meeting for 19 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the directors’ report, audited financial statements, and auditor’s report for the year ended 31 December 2025. The meeting will also consider the re-election of executive directors and the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, reinforcing continuity in both management and external oversight.

Shareholders will vote on granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, and to make related offers and instruments such as warrants or convertible securities. This mandate, a common corporate finance tool in Hong Kong, would give the board flexibility to raise capital or pursue strategic transactions, potentially affecting future dilution, capital structure, and the company’s ability to respond quickly to market opportunities.

More about Huayi Tencent Entertainment Co., Ltd.

Hony Media Group, listed in Hong Kong, operates in the media and entertainment sector and is incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability. The company is overseen by a board that includes executive directors and engages external auditors for its financial reporting, reflecting a conventional listed-company governance structure focused on shareholder oversight and compliance with Hong Kong market regulations.

Sanyo Denki Co., Ltd. ( (JP:6516) ) has shared an update.

Sanyo Denki has disclosed that it received a shareholder proposal ahead of its 124th Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders scheduled for June 2026, submitted by Intertrust Trustees (Cayman) Limited in its capacity as trustee of JAPAN‑UP and by Strategic Capital, Inc. The board plans to carefully review the proposal, which calls for electing one director and amending the Articles of Incorporation on directors’ terms of office, capital policy review, stock splits, and the total number of authorized shares, signaling potential changes to governance and capital structure that could affect shareholder rights and the company’s capital management strategy.

data.title: African Gold Director Sells Direct Stake but Retains Large Indirect Holding
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from African Gold Ltd. ( (AU:A1G) ).

African Gold Ltd. has reported a change in the holdings of director Mathew O’Hara, who sold his directly held stake of 42,004 ordinary shares in an on-market transaction at $1.025 per share. Following the sale, O’Hara retains a substantial indirect interest through the O’Hara Investment Trust, which still holds over 7 million ordinary shares and 5 million options, indicating that his overall economic exposure to the company remains significant despite the disposal of his personal shareholding.

The transaction, which did not occur during a closed trading period, suggests routine portfolio or personal financial management rather than a complete exit from the stock, given O’Hara’s continuing position via the trust and options. For investors, the notice clarifies that while the director has reduced his directly held shares to zero, his aligned interests with other shareholders persist through his large indirect holdings, limiting the potential governance or confidence impact that might arise from a full divestment.

More about African Gold Ltd.

African Gold Ltd. is an Australia-listed mining company focused on the exploration and development of gold assets. The company is part of the broader resources sector and its securities are traded on the ASX, where it is subject to ongoing disclosure requirements, including reporting changes in directors’ interests.

Average Trading Volume: 2,318,818

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$609.3M

data.title: Elica Bets on Proprietary Induction Tech as It Repositions as a Full Cooking Player
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Elica S.p.A. ( (IT:ELC) ) has issued an update.

Elica is using EuroCucina 2026 at Milan’s Salone del Mobile to showcase its evolution into a full cooking specialist, unveiling its first range of induction hobs built on proprietary ID Technology and the new Matrix user interface. Developed through a €2 million, three-year R&D effort, this in-house hardware and software platform is designed to give Elica greater control over innovation, strengthen its competitive positioning and support a scalable pipeline of future products.

The new hobs promise a more flexible, ergonomic and quiet cooking experience, with precise temperature-based control, automatic modes and a high-definition pixel-matrix display that enables intuitive graphics, animations and messages. Alongside these launches, Elica presents upgrades to its flagship Lhov all‑in‑one cooking system and advanced lighting solution Luna, reinforcing a premium, design‑driven vision of the kitchen as an open, borderless environment that blends creativity, technology and smart connectivity.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:ELC) stock is a Hold with a EUR1.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Elica S.p.A. stock, see the IT:ELC Stock Forecast page.

More about Elica S.p.A.

Elica S.p.A. is an Italian company operating in the cooking and kitchen appliances industry, known for its design-led range hoods and integrated cooking solutions. The group focuses on premium, technologically advanced products that blend aesthetics, smart functions and high construction quality for global kitchen markets.
